    [Comment 9] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 27 Jan 2026 15:26:00 +0100):

    SASS compiler

    Update to angular 19, brought sass@1.85.0 which marks usages of "@import" in scss files as deprecated. As we were using it extensively build- and browser consoles are spammed with warnings.

    Was able to migrate to new syntax in stylesheets of components, however not in global stylesheets, which are importing 3rd party stylesheets we have no control over (boostrap4, bnotk/ds-ng).

        Hi everyone,

        ticket got renamed to Angular 19.

        Filip Misiorek & Oleksii Samusenko your T&M efforts for analysis please still book on this ticket. I'll update the description of above's ticket soon to fit to this task, once confirmed by [Comment 14] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 14:21:58 +0100):

        ag-grid has quite extensive list of breaking changes, accommodating them will take some effort:
        https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/upgrading-to-ag-grid-32/#breaking-changes
         
        Should be on UVZ:
        node.js v22.12.0
        "typescript": "5.9.0", (5.8.0 with Angular 19.2)
         
        From Angular documentation  RxJS should be set to ^6.5.3 or ^7.4.0, so we are fine with that because we are now using rxjs 7.4.0 version.
         
        This part is basing on:

implementation_steps: 1. Update Node.js version to the LTS release required by Angular 19 (modify .nvmrc and CI pipelines).
2. Upgrade TypeScript to the version compatible with Angular 19 (update tsconfig.json and package.json).
3. Replace Angular core packages from 18 to 19 using ng update (run ng update @angular/core@19 @angular/cli@19).
4. Update Pattern Library (bnotk/ds-ng) to version 12.6.0 in package.json and run npm install.
5. Align ng-bootstrap to the version that includes the UVZUSLNVV-5824 patch (apply patch file, update package.json).
6. Upgrade ng-select and ng-option-highlight to the latest compatible versions (modify imports in components using them).
7. Update ag-grid-angular and ag-grid-community to versions supporting Angular 19 (adjust grid options if API changes).
8. Refactor any deprecated Angular 18 APIs found in the affected components (e.g., StaticContentController, JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl).
9. Verify that the vertical action bar component still compiles and functions with PL 12.6.0 (run Storybook checks).
10. Remove leftover files, modules, or CSS classes that belong exclusively to Angular 18 or PL 11.3.1 (search and delete).
11. Run full application build and resolve any compilation errors (update import paths, adjust typings).
12. Execute unit and integration test suites; fix failing tests caused by the upgrade.
13. Update CI/CD pipelines to use the new Node.js, TypeScript, and Angular versions.
14. Document the upgrade steps, version changes, and any manual migration notes in the project README.

unit_tests: Compile all Angular modules with Angular 19 and assert no TypeScript errors.
Test that ng-bootstrap components render correctly after applying the UVZUSLNVV-5824 patch.
Validate that ng-select dropdowns work with the upgraded ng-select version.
integration_tests: End‑to‑end test of the vertical action bar through Storybook to ensure visual and functional integrity.
API integration test for each discovered interface to confirm that backend controllers still respond after the frontend upgrade.

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (cors in ActionRestService).
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for state‑changing operations (csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (cors in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (cors in JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl).
Apply pre_authorize security pattern where appropriate (pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService).
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotation to prevent null values (not_null on DeedEntry).
Add @Valid annotation to enforce nested validation (valid on DeedEntryRestService).
Add @NotBlank annotation to required string fields (not_blank on KmConfig).
Add @Min annotation to enforce minimum numeric values (min on KmConfig).
Add @Max annotation to enforce maximum numeric values (max on KmConfig).
error_handling: Create or reuse a custom RuntimeException for domain‑specific errors (RuntimeException custom_exception).
Add @ExceptionHandler for generic Exception in a global exception handler (Exception exception_handler).
Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpClientErrorException in a global exception handler (HttpClientErrorException exception_handler).
Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException in a global exception handler (HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException exception_handler).

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The upgrade touches core framework versions, multiple third‑party UI libraries, and requires removal of deprecated code while preserving the vertical action bar, leading to moderate effort across many components.
risks: Breaking changes in Angular 19 may cause runtime errors in legacy components.
Incompatible versions of ng-bootstrap or ag‑grid could require additional code adjustments.
Residual Angular 18 specific code might be missed, leading to hidden bugs.
CI/CD pipeline failures due to mismatched Node.js or TypeScript versions.
